Extracting all Cities and their population

Einige Landkreise sind nicht unter den Namen zu finden. Diese wurden händisch nachgetragen, inclusive OSM ID.

Diese Landkreise wurden aufgrund von Fehlern aussortiert.

error_landkreise = [
    'Aichach-Friedberg',
    'Amberg-Sulzbach',
    'Bad Tölz-Wolfratshausen',
    'Berchtesgadener Land',
    'Dingolfing-Landau',
    'Erlangen-Höchstadt',
    'Haßberge',
    'Neuburg-Schrobenhausen',
    'Rhön-Grabfeld',
    'Straubing-Bogen',
    'Unterallgäu',
    'Weilheim-Schongau',
    'Darmstadt-Dieburg',
    'Limburg-Weilburg',
    'Grafschaft Bentheim',
    'Mayen-Koblenz',
    'Südliche Weinstraße',
    'Vulkaneifel',
    'Eichsfeld',
    'Wartburgkreis (incl. Eisenach)']

def get_json(request_string, by_osmid=False):
    error_landkreise = []
    filepath = Path("out", "landkreisdata",
                    "boundary_" + "{}".format(request_string))
    if filepath.is_dir():
        json_data = geopandas.read_file(filepath)
    else:
        # if landkreis in error_landkreise:
        #     return None
        try:
            if by_osmid:
                request_string = "R{}".format(request_string)
                json_data = ox.geocoder.geocode_to_gdf(
                    request_string, buffer_dist=200, by_osmid=by_osmid)
            else:
                json_data = ox.geocoder.geocode_to_gdf(
                    request_string, buffer_dist=200)
            json_data.to_file(filepath)
        except ValueError as e:
            print(e)
            error_landkreise.append(request_string)
            json_data = None

    return json_data, error_landkreise

jsondata = {}
error_landkreise = []
for bundesland, landkreise in bundesländer_landkreise.items():
    print(bundesland, landkreise)

    for landkreis in landkreise:
        x = None

        # print(x.shape)
        landkreis_string = landkreis
        landkreis_data, tmp_error_landkreise = get_json(landkreis_string)
        error_landkreise.extend(tmp_error_landkreise)

        jsondata[landkreis_string] = landkreis_data

        # sometimes the above only fetches the city with the same name not the landkreis
        if landkreis.split(" ")[0] == "Landkreis":
            continue

        real_landkreis_string = "Landkreis " + landkreis
        prefix_data, _ = get_json(real_landkreis_string)

        if prefix_data is None:
            continue

        # throw away overlapping stuff.

        newergeometry = prefix_data.iloc[0]["geometry"]
        if landkreis_data is None:
            continue
        oldergeometry = landkreis_data.iloc[0]["geometry"]

        if (oldergeometry.disjoint(newergeometry)):
            continue
        if (oldergeometry.contains(newergeometry)):
            continue

        if newergeometry.covers(oldergeometry):
            # override the old one if we have a city included in the landkreisdata
            jsondata[landkreis_string] = None

            # print("No OverLapp for {}".format(landkreis))
        print("added {}".format(real_landkreis_string))
        jsondata[real_landkreis_string] = prefix_data

for landkreis, id in extra_landkreise.items():
    landkreis_string = id
    landkreis_data, tmp_error_landkreise = get_json(
        landkreis_string, by_osmid=True)
    error_landkreise.extend(tmp_error_landkreise)
    jsondata[landkreis_string] = landkreis_data

Example: {‘place_id’: 13256685, ‘licence’: ‘Data © OpenStreetMap contributors, ODbL 1.0. https://osm.org/copyright’, ‘osm_type’: ‘node’, ‘osm_id’: 1327914605, ‘boundingbox’: [‘51.6544983’, ‘51.6944983’, ‘14.4211008’, ‘14.4611008’], ‘lat’: ‘51.6744983’, ‘lon’: ‘14.4411008’, ‘display_name’: ‘Laubsdorf, Neuhausen/Spree, Spree-Neiße, Brandenburg, 03058, Deutschland’, ‘class’: ‘place’, ‘type’: ‘village’, ‘importance’: 0.385, ‘icon’: ‘https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/ui/mapicons//poi_place_village.p.20.png’}

def make_geodataframe_from_dict(d, bbox):
    bbox = [
        float(bbox[0]),
        float(bbox[1]),
        float(bbox[2]),
        float(bbox[3]),
    ]
    geometry = shapely.geometry.Polygon(
        [
            [bbox[2], bbox[0]],
            [bbox[2], bbox[1]],
            [bbox[3], bbox[1]],
            [bbox[3], bbox[0]],
        ]
    )
    my_list = [
        geometry,
        bbox[1], bbox[0], bbox[3], bbox[2],
        d["place_id"], "relation", d["osm_id"],
        d["lat"], d["lon"], d["display_name"],
        d["class"], d["type"], d["importance"]
    ]
    columns = ["geometry", 'bbox_north', 'bbox_south', 'bbox_east', 'bbox_west',
               'place_id', 'osm_type', 'osm_id', 'lat', 'lon', 'display_name', 'class',
               'type', 'importance']

    # ESPG:4326
    df = geopandas.GeoDataFrame([my_list], columns=columns, crs="EPSG:4326")

    return df

def node_list_to_df(nodelist):
    df_list = []
    for node in nodelist:
        # print(node)
        df_list.append([node.id, node.lat, node.lon, node.tags])
    df = pd.DataFrame(df_list, columns=["id", "lat", "lon", "tags"])
    return df

def df_to_node_list(df):
    node_list = []
    for row in df.itertuples():
        # print(row)
        try:
            tags = row.tags
        except Exception as e:
            print(e)
            try:
                print(row.name)
            except Exception as e:
                print(e)
            tags = {}
        # print(int(row.id), float(row.lat), float(row.lon))
        d = {}
        if tags is None:
            tags = {}
        else:
            try:
                d = eval(tags)
            except Exception as e:
                print(e)
                print(type(tags))
                # raise e

        if row.id is not None:
            node_list.append(overpy.Node(node_id=int(row.id), lat=float(
                row.lat), lon=float(row.lon), attributes=[], tags=d))

    return node_list

# overpy request
def fetch_all_cities_in(bbox, sleep=0):
    # print(float(bbox[0]), float(bbox[1]), float(bbox[2]), float(bbox[3]))
    request = """[out:json]
  [timeout:120]
  ;
  (
    node
      ["place"="village"]
      ({0},{1},{2},{3});
    node
      ["place"="hamlet"]
      ({0},{1},{2},{3});
    node
      ["place"="city"]
      ({0},{1},{2},{3});
    node
      ["place"="town"]
      ({0},{1},{2},{3});
    node
      ["place"="suburb"]
      ({0},{1},{2},{3});
  );
  out;
  >;
  out skel qt;""".format(float(bbox[0]), float(bbox[1]), float(bbox[2]), float(bbox[3]))
    print(request)
    api = overpy.Overpass()
    try:
        time.sleep(sleep)
        result = api.query(request)
        return result
    except Exception as e:
        print(e)
        if e is TimeoutError:
            if sleep > 150:
                return None
            sleep += 30
            return fetch_all_cities_in(bbox, sleep)

def get_all_cities_in(landkreis, box):
    filepath = Path("out", "landkreisdata",
                    "{}".format(landkreis) + ".csv")
    if filepath.is_file():
        all_cities_in_bbox = load_all_cities(filepath)
        print("Loading: " + str(filepath))
    else:
        result = fetch_all_cities_in(box, 30)
        node_list = [node for node in result.nodes]
        save_all_cities(node_list, filepath)
        all_cities_in_bbox = node_list
        print("Fetched: {} with {} cities".format(filepath, len(node_list)))
    return all_cities_in_bbox

def load_all_cities(filepath):
    df = pd.read_csv(filepath)
    return df_to_node_list(df)

def save_all_cities(node_list, filepath):
    # print(node_list)
    df = node_list_to_df(node_list)
    # print(df)
    df.to_csv(filepath)

def filter_cities(list_of_cities, geometry):
    points = []
    if list_of_cities is None:
        return
    for entry in list_of_cities:
        if geometry.contains(shapely.geometry.Point(entry.lon, entry.lat))[0]:
            points.append(entry)
    return points

#     return df_cities

def box_around(center, radius):
    corner1 = center[0] + converter.convert_meter_to_lat(radius, center[0])
    corner2 = center[0] - converter.convert_meter_to_lat(radius, center[0])
    corner3 = center[1] + converter.convert_meter_to_lon(radius, center[0])
    corner4 = center[1] - converter.convert_meter_to_lon(radius, center[0])
    return [corner1, corner2, corner3, corner4]

def geometry_from_box(bbox):
    bbox = [
        float(bbox[0]),
        float(bbox[1]),
        float(bbox[2]),
        float(bbox[3]),
    ]
    geometry = shapely.geometry.Polygon(
        [
            [bbox[2], bbox[0]],
            [bbox[2], bbox[1]],
            [bbox[3], bbox[1]],
            [bbox[3], bbox[0]],
        ]
    )
    return geometry

def make_custom_boxes(cities):
    boxes = []
    for city in cities:
        center = (float(city.lat), float(city.lon))
        # print(city)
        # print(city.tags)
        # print(type(city.tags))
        if city.tags is not None:
            vilage_type = city.tags.get("place")
        radius = None
        if vilage_type == "hamlet":
            radius = 500
        if vilage_type == "village":
            radius = 500
        if vilage_type == "suburb":
            radius = 1000
        if vilage_type == "town":
            radius = 1000
        if vilage_type == "city":
            radius = 1000
        if radius is None:
            radius = 1000

        bbox = box_around(center, radius)
        boxes.append(bbox)

    return boxes

def prep_gdf(gdf, boxes, points):
    gdf["box"] = boxes
    gdf["geometry"] = gdf["box"].apply(geometry_from_box)
    gdf["name"] = [point.tags.get("name") for point in points]
    # gdf["pop"] = [) if point.tags.get("population") is not None else 0 for point in points]
    pop = []
    for point in points:
        this_pop = point.tags.get("population")
        if this_pop is None:
            pop.append(0)
        else:
            try:
                pop.append(int(point.tags.get("population")))
            except ValueError:
                # ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10: '835 (2014)'
                pop.append(0)

    gdf["pop"] = pop
    # gdf["pop"].fillna(0)
    gdf["color"] = ["green" if pop > 1000 else "red" for pop in gdf["pop"].to_list()]
    return gdf

# unclean_name= "Hej däß„?"
# name.encode("ascii", errors="ignore").decode()
# name = re.sub(r'[^\x00-\x7f]',r' ',unclean_name)
# name.replace("?" , "")

def clean_name(unclean_name, printable=None):
    if printable is None:
        printable = set(string.printable)
        printable.update(set(("ä", "Ä", "ü", "Ü", "ö", "Ö", "ß", "è", "é")))
    name = ''.join(filter(lambda x: x in printable, unclean_name))
    name = name.replace("/", ";")
    name = name.replace("\\", ";")
    name = name.replace("?", "")
    name = name.replace("\"", "")
    return name

def prep_files(gdf):
    files = []
    lat1 = []
    lat2 = []
    lon1 = []
    lon2 = []
    printable = set(string.printable)
    printable.update(set(("ä", "Ä", "ü", "Ü", "ö", "Ö", "ß", "è", "é")))

    for row in gdf.itertuples():
        if row.pop != 0:
            continue
        box = gdf["box"].iloc[row.Index]
        if row.name is None:
            print("Error with Row, didn't have a name")
            print(row)
            continue
        if type(row.name) is float:
            continue
        # clean the name from unprintable chars
        name = clean_name(row.name, printable)
        # print(name)
        files.append(Path("csvs", "{}-100m.csv".format(name)))
        # print(box)
        lat1.append(box[0])
        lon1.append(box[3])
        lat2.append(box[1])
        lon2.append(box[2])
    return files, lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2

def get_pop(gdf):
    for row in gdf.itertuples():
        if row.pop != 0:
            continue
        # print(row)
        # print(row.name)
        if row.name is None:
            print("Error with Row, didn't have a name")
            print(row)
            continue
        if type(row.name) is float:
            continue
        name = clean_name(row.name)
        gridcsv = Path("csvs", "{}-100m.csv".format(name))
        pop = extract.get_pop_total_100(gridcsv)
        print(row.name, pop)
        gdf.at[row.Index, "pop"] = pop
    return gdf

def calc_landkreis_cities(landkreis, jsondata, filepath):
    data = jsondata.get(landkreis, None)
    if data is None:
        print("No data for: {}".format(landkreis))
        return
    box = jsondata[landkreis].geometry.bounds
    # print(box)
    all_cities_in_bbox = get_all_cities_in(
        landkreis,
        [
            box.miny,
            box.minx,
            box.maxy,
            box.maxx,
        ]
    )
    points = filter_cities(all_cities_in_bbox, jsondata[landkreis].geometry)
    boxes = make_custom_boxes(points)
    gdf = geopandas.GeoDataFrame(
        columns=["box", "geometry", "name", "tags", "pop", "color"], crs="EPSG:4326")
    gdf = prep_gdf(gdf, boxes, points)
    # print(gdf)
    # gdf cant save lists so save it as string and eval on load
    # also cant save empty frames
    if not gdf.empty:
        # convert boxes for saving to string
        gdf["box"] = gdf["box"].apply(lambda x: str(x))
        gdf.to_file(filepath)
        # but after saving we want to return them as normal lists again...
        gdf["box"].apply(lambda x: eval(x))
    return gdf

def get_landkreis_cities(landkreis, jsondata, filepath):
    if filepath.is_dir():
        print("Have cities of {} loading...".format(landkreis))
        gdf = geopandas.read_file(filepath)
        gdf["box"] = gdf["box"].apply(lambda x: eval(x))
        # print(type(gdf["box"].iloc[0]))
    else:
        gdf = calc_landkreis_cities(landkreis, jsondata, filepath)
    return gdf

def get_bundesland_cities(jsondata, landkreise):
    landkreisdata = {}
    for landkreis in landkreise:
        filepath = Path("out", "landkreisdata",
                        "cities_" + "{}".format(landkreis))
        print("Fetching cities for landkreis: {}".format(landkreis))
        gdf = get_landkreis_cities(landkreis, jsondata, filepath)
        if gdf is None:
            continue
        landkreisdata[landkreis] = gdf

    return landkreisdata

def extract_city_files(landkreisdata, landkreise):
    for landkreis in landkreise:
        gdf = landkreisdata.get(landkreis, None)
        if gdf is None:
            continue
        files, lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2 = prep_files(gdf)
        print("extract cities for {}".format(landkreis))
        extract.extract_place_easy_multiple(files,
                                            file_input=Path(
                                                "csv_Bevoelkerung_100m_Gitter", "lat-lon-deci-Einw.csv"),
                                            lat1=lat1, lon1=lon1, lat2=lat2, lon2=lon2)

def get_pop_bundesland(landkreisdata, landkreise):

    filepath_pop_status = Path("out", "landkreisdata", "status.csv")
    if filepath_pop_status.is_file():
        with open(filepath_pop_status, "r") as f:
            pop_status = json.load(f)
            print("Loading Status")
            type(pop_status)
            print(pop_status)
    else:
        pop_status = {}

    for landkreis in landkreise:
        if pop_status.get(landkreis, False):
            print("Have pop data for {} skipping...".format(landkreis))
            continue

        filepath = Path("out", "landkreisdata",
                        "cities_" + "{}".format(landkreis))

        # if filepath.is_dir():
        #     print("Have pop data for {} skipping...".format(landkreis))

        print("getting population data for {}".format(landkreis))
        gdf = landkreisdata.get(landkreis, None)
        if gdf is None:
            continue
        gdf = get_pop(gdf)
        gdf["color"] = ["green" if pop >
                        1000 else "red" for pop in gdf["pop"].to_list()]
        # can't save lists
        # print(gdf)
        if not gdf.empty:
            gdf["box"] = gdf["box"].apply(lambda x: str(x))
            gdf.to_file(filepath)
            # but after saving we want to return them as normal lists again...
            gdf["box"].apply(lambda x: eval(x))
        pop_status[landkreis] = True
        landkreisdata[landkreis] = gdf

    print("saving status")
    status_json = json.dumps(pop_status, indent=4)
    with open(filepath_pop_status, "w") as f:
        f.write(status_json)

    return landkreisdata

def add_prefix_landkreise(jsondata, landkreise):
    prefix_landkreise = []
    for landkreis in landkreise:
        prefixed = "Landkreis {}".format(landkreis)
        pefixed_data = jsondata.get(prefixed, None)
        if prefix_data is not None:
            prefix_landkreise.append(prefixed)
    prefix_landkreise.extend(landkreise)
    return prefix_landkreise

bundesland_data = {}
# TODO mkdir Path("..", "out", "landkreisdata")

for i in range(0, 10):
    try:
        for bundesland, landkreise in bundesländer_landkreise.items():

            with_prefixes = add_prefix_landkreise(jsondata, landkreise)
            print(bundesland, with_prefixes)
            # extract_places()
            landkreisdata = get_bundesland_cities(jsondata, with_prefixes)
            extract_city_files(landkreisdata, with_prefixes)
            landkreisdata = get_pop_bundesland(landkreisdata, with_prefixes)
            bundesland_data[bundesland] = landkreisdata
    except Exception as e:
        print(e)
        continue

# overpy.Node(node_id=31166813, lat=51.8555930, lon=14.0434258)

def plot_landkreis(landkreis, gdf, feature_group_bundesland):
    landkreis_map = folium.Map(location=[50.915066, 13.346332],
                               zoom_start=8)

    json_data = jsondata.get(landkreis, None)
    if json_data is None:
        return None, feature_group_bundesland

    # toggle for each landkreis in bundeslandmap
    feature_group_landkreis = folium.FeatureGroup(landkreis)
    # toggle for landkreis boundry for landkreis map
    feature_group_landkreis_boundary = folium.FeatureGroup(landkreis)

    # add the outlines of the landkreis to:
    folium.GeoJson(geopandas.GeoDataFrame(json_data, crs="EPSG:4326")).add_to(
        feature_group_landkreis_boundary)
    folium.GeoJson(geopandas.GeoDataFrame(
        json_data, crs="EPSG:4326")).add_to(feature_group_landkreis)
    folium.GeoJson(geopandas.GeoDataFrame(json_data, crs="EPSG:4326")).add_to(
        feature_group_bundesland)

    feature_group_landkreis_boundary.add_to(landkreis_map)

    for row in gdf.itertuples():

        # toggle for each city
        feature_group_city = folium.FeatureGroup(row.name)

        box = gdf["box"].iloc[row.Index]
        if type(box) == str:
            # I think boxes are saved wrong when the entry node has a pop data assigned
            box = eval(box)

        folium.Rectangle([
            [box[0], box[2]],
            [box[1], box[3]],
        ],
            popup=row.pop,
            fill_color=row.color,
            color=row.color
        ).add_to(feature_group_city)

        folium.Rectangle([
            [box[0], box[2]],
            [box[1], box[3]],
        ],
            popup=row.pop,
            fill_color=row.color,
            color=row.color
        ).add_to(feature_group_landkreis)

        folium.Rectangle([
            [box[0], box[2]],
            [box[1], box[3]],
        ],
            popup=row.pop,
            fill_color=row.color,
            color=row.color
        ).add_to(feature_group_bundesland)

        feature_group_city.add_to(landkreis_map)

    folium.LayerControl().add_to(landkreis_map)
    landkreis_map_filepath = Path("maps", "Gemeinde_{}.html".format(landkreis))
    landkreis_map.save(str(landkreis_map_filepath))
    return feature_group_landkreis, feature_group_bundesland

def eval_landkreis(landkreis, gdf, x):
    gdff = gdf[gdf["pop"] > 1000]
    over = len(gdff.index)
    under = len(gdf.index) - over

    x[landkreis] = [over, under]
    return x

def plot_bundesland(bundesland, landkreisdata, deutschland_map=None):
    # TODO get the propper location
    if landkreisdata is None:
        return deutschland_map
    bundesland_map = folium.Map(location=[50.915066, 13.346332],
                                zoom_start=8)

    feature_group_bundesland = folium.FeatureGroup(bundesland, show=False)
    for landkreis, gdf in landkreisdata.items():
        feature_group_landkreis, feature_group_bundesland = plot_landkreis(
            landkreis, gdf, feature_group_bundesland)
        if feature_group_landkreis is not None:
            feature_group_landkreis.add_to(bundesland_map)

    if deutschland_map is not None:
        feature_group_bundesland.add_to(deutschland_map)

    folium.LayerControl().add_to(bundesland_map)

    bundesland_map_filepath = Path(
        "maps", "Bundesland_{}.html".format(bundesland))
    bundesland_map.save(str(bundesland_map_filepath))

    return deutschland_map

def eval_bundesland(bundesland, landkreisdata, x=None):
    # TODO get the propper location
    if landkreisdata is None:
        return x

    for landkreis, gdf in landkreisdata.items():
        x = eval_landkreis(landkreis, gdf, x)

    bundesland_tuple = [0, 0]
    for landkreis, _ in landkreisdata.items():
        landkreis_tuple = x.get(landkreis, None)
        if landkreis_tuple is not None:
            bundesland_tuple[0] += landkreis_tuple[0]
            bundesland_tuple[1] += landkreis_tuple[1]
    x[bundesland] = bundesland_tuple
    return x

x = {}
land_tuple = [0, 0]
for bundesland, landkreise in bundesländer_landkreise.items():
    x = eval_bundesland(bundesland, bundesland_data[bundesland], x)
    bundesland_tuple = x.get(bundesland, None)
    if bundesland_tuple is not None:
        print(bundesland_tuple)
        land_tuple[0] += bundesland_tuple[0]
        land_tuple[1] += bundesland_tuple[1]

# for landkreis in special_landkreise:
x = eval_bundesland(handpick_name, bundesland_data[handpick_name], x)
bundesland_tuple = x.get(handpick_name, None)
if bundesland_tuple is not None:
    print(bundesland_tuple)
    land_tuple[0] += bundesland_tuple[0]
    land_tuple[1] += bundesland_tuple[1]
x["Deutschland"] = land_tuple

df = pd.DataFrame(x).T
df.columns = ["over", "under"]
filepath = Path("maps", "Gemeinden-1k-{}.csv".format("Deutschland"))
df.to_csv(filepath)

over     0

Name: Deutschland, dtype: int64

deutschland_map = folium.Map(location=[50.915066, 13.346332],
                             zoom_start=8)
for bundesland, landkreise in bundesländer_landkreise.items():
    deutschland_map = plot_bundesland(
        bundesland, bundesland_data[bundesland], deutschland_map)

# for landkreis in special_landkreise:
deutschland_map = plot_bundesland(
    handpick_name, bundesland_data[handpick_name], deutschland_map)


folium.LayerControl().add_to(deutschland_map)
deutschland_map_filepath = Path("maps", "deutschland.html")
deutschland_map.save(str(deutschland_map_filepath))

#     ],).add_to(m)
# m

def fetch_tags(ids):
    if len(ids) <= 0:
        return

    nodestring = "[out:json];node(id:"
    for id in ids:
        nodestring += "{}, ".format(id)
    nodestring = nodestring[:-2]
    nodestring += ");out;"
    print(nodestring)
    api = overpy.Overpass()
    try:
        time.sleep(10)
        result = api.query(nodestring)
        return result
    except Exception as e:
        print(e)

# "Biberach"

def get_tags_landkreis(landkreis):
    filepath = Path("out", "landkreisdata", landkreis + ".csv")
    print(filepath)
    if not filepath.is_file():
        return
    df = pd.read_csv(filepath)
    if "tags" in df.columns:
        return
    ids = df["id"].to_list()
    # print(ids)
    result = fetch_tags(ids)
    if result is None:
        return
    tag_dict = {}
    for node in result.nodes:
        tag_dict[node.id] = node.tags
    # print(tag_dict)
    df["tags"] = df["id"].map(tag_dict)
    # df["tags"] = tag_list
    # print(df)
    df.to_csv(filepath)
    time.sleep(10)

                           zoom_start=10)

folium.GeoJson(geopandas.GeoDataFrame(
    jsondata["Hof"], crs="EPSG:4326")).add_to(landkreis_map)

x = ox.geocoder.geocode_to_gdf("Wunsiedel i. Fichtelgebirge", buffer_dist=200)
x

landkreis_map = folium.Map(location=[51.631003, 14.033855],
                           zoom_start=10)

    x, crs="EPSG:4326")).add_to(landkreis_map)
